pi_items.source: Calculated from CSV data in setup_task.sh: sum of 14 sleep values / 14 nights = 93.8 / 14 = 6.70
Counted values < 7 from Time Asleep column: 6.8, 6.2, 6.5, 5.8, 5.5, 6.0, 5.9, 6.9, 6.7 = 9 nights
Counted rows in CSV data embedded in setup_task.sh (rows 15-28 inclusive)

pi_items.verified_value: (E/D)*100 where E=Time Asleep (hrs), D=Time in Bed (hrs)
pi_items.source: README.md line 17 describes formula; setup_task.sh line 106 shows expected pattern =(E3/D3)*100
Calculated from first row: (6.8 hrs asleep / 7.5 hrs in bed) × 100 = 90.67%

pi_items.metadata_value: Date, Bedtime, Wake Time, Time in Bed (hrs), Time Asleep (hrs), Caffeine After 2pm, Screen Time Before Bed (mins)
pi_items.verified_value: Exact structure confirmed from CSV in setup_task.sh
pi_items.source: setup_task.sh lines 14-28, verified 7 columns with correct headers
Data is hardcoded in setup_task.sh using cat heredoc, not from external source

privileged_info_summary: The sleep tracking dataset contains 14 nights of synthetic data with an average sleep duration of 6.70 hours (claimed ≈ 6.8 hours, within tolerance). However, the count of nights with insufficient sleep (<7 hours) is 9 nights, not the claimed 5 nights. The data includes sleep duration, time in bed, caffeine consumption, and screen time metrics. The first night has 90.67% sleep efficiency (6.8 hours asleep / 7.5 hours in bed).
